---
title: "Info"
---

## Real-Time Analytics

Code: 222891-D

Spring semester 2025/2026,
SGH Warsaw School of Economics

Detailed course description is available in the [syllabus](sylabus.qmd).
Recommended reading is available in the [books](books.qmd) section.

## Schedule

### Lectures

Lectures are conducted in a hybrid format.
Attendance is optional, with in-person sessions held in Aula VI, Building G.

1. ~~23.02.2026 Lecture 1 -- 11:40--13:20~~
2. ~~02.03.2026 Lecture 2 -- 11:40--13:20~~
3. ~~09.03.2026 Lecture 3 -- 11:40--13:20~~
4. <del>16.03.2026 Lecture 4 -- 11:40--13:20</del>
5. <del>23.03.2026 Lecture 5 -- 11:40--13:20</del>

### Test

Lectures conclude with a test during the final lecture (Lecture 5).

- Format: 20 questions, 30 minutes
- Platform: MS Teams
- Passing threshold: more than 13 points -- this is a prerequisite for participating in the labs.

### Labs and Assessment

After completing the exercises, homework assignments must be submitted via MS Teams.
Each missing homework lowers the final grade by 0.5.

#### Project

Projects must be completed in groups of no more than 5 people.

Project requirements:

- The project should address a real business problem that can be solved using data processed in real time (batch processing for model training is also acceptable).
- Data should be ingested into Apache Kafka for further processing and analysis.
- Any programming language may be used for each component of the project.
- Business Intelligence (BI) tools may be utilized.
- Data sources may include any API, synthetically generated data, IoT devices, etc.

## Technology

To participate in the course, you need to be familiar with the following technologies:

1. [Git](info.qmd#git)
2. Python, Jupyter Notebook, JupyterLab, Google Colab
3. [Docker and Docker Compose](info.qmd#docker)
4. **Apache Spark**, **Apache Kafka**
